AC-QGATE-01.why: Pure sampling-rate logic — §3.2.2 QUALITY_GATE_SAMPLE_RATE cadence; seeded-RNG count assertion over 1000 entries, no LLM call exercised.

AC-QGATE-02.why: always-check for decision->final forces the Haiku entailment call (§3.2.2 'a decision going final ... always-check'); oracle asserts the gate entailment call is actually issued at rate=0.0.

AC-QGATE-02-NEG.why: NEG counterpart asserts ZERO API calls when the feature flag disables the gate — pure guard/branch logic, no LLM exercised (feature-gate suppresses even always-check).

AC-QGATE-03.why: always-check for reversibility=irreversible fires the Haiku entailment call regardless of sample draw (§3.2.2 'an irreversible reversibility'); oracle asserts gate call count==1.

AC-QGATE-03-NEG.why: NEG: a reversible entry at rate=0.0 issues ZERO gate calls — pure not-always-checked branch logic, no LLM call made.

AC-QGATE-04.why: always-check for a contradicts link fires the Haiku entailment call (§3.2.2 'a contradicts link'); oracle asserts gate call count==1 despite rate=0.0.

AC-QGATE-04-NEG.why: NEG: a plain entry with no always-check flag at rate=0.0 issues ZERO gate calls — pure suppression branch, no LLM exercised.

AC-QGATE-05.why: Apply-then-gate ordering property (§3.2.2 'sampled delta has already been applied ... gate runs beside the loop'); timestamp/ordering assertion on instrumented applier, verifiable without a real LLM.

AC-QGATE-06.why: Static AST scan of the prompt-builder asserting context is exactly window+entry (§3.2.2 'reads only the transcript window + the emitted entry text'); no call issued — pure static analysis.

AC-QGATE-07.why: Model-resolver / env-var logic (§3.2.2 PROXY_MODEL_QUALITY_GATE, default claude-haiku-4-5) plus source grep for hardcoded literals — resolver returns a string, no LLM call.

AC-QGATE-07-NEG.why: Asserts the entailment call is issued with the configured model field (no silent downgrade); oracle inspects the captured call_external request to the gate model, exercising the real gate call path.

AC-QGATE-08.why: Response-parser unit over fixtures — parses {grounded,reason}, parse-failure -> grounded=false (§3.2.2 gate answers {grounded:bool,reason:str}); fixtures only, no live call.

AC-QGATE-08-NEG.why: Cassette-mocked gate entailment call returning grounded=true drives the no-escalation branch; classed same as positive gate-call criteria since it exercises the entailment call at call_external.

AC-QGATE-09.why: On grounded=false, re-run that extraction on the next tier — Sonnet (§3.2.2 'PROXY_MODEL_SCRIBE -> Sonnet-class'); oracle asserts exactly one Sonnet-class extraction call over the same window.

AC-QGATE-09-NEG.why: NEG pair of QGATE-09: cassette-mocked grounded=true gate call, assert zero Sonnet escalation calls — same escalation LLM flow, same class as its positive pair.

AC-QGATE-10.why: Sonnet correction applied as PatchOp attributed to the gate, original superseded-not-erased (§3.2.2 'replace the entry via the normal applier path, a patch, superseded-not-erased'); both gate + Sonnet calls cassette-mocked.

AC-QGATE-10-NEG.why: NEG pair of QGATE-10: cassette-mocked Sonnet re-extraction returns empty, assert no patch applied — same escalation call flow, same class as its positive pair.

AC-QGATE-11.why: End-to-end miss trace (grounded=false gate call + Sonnet correction, both cassette-mocked) asserting exactly one quality-gate-miss record with window span/Haiku entry/Sonnet correction (§3.2.2 log the miss); exercises the gate+escalation calls.

AC-QGATE-11-NEG.why: NEG pair of QGATE-11: cassette-mocked grounded=false + Sonnet null, assert miss still logged once — same gate+escalation call flow, same class as its positive pair.

AC-QGATE-12.why: Miss-rate = M/N derivation from transcript-plane records (§3.2.2 cascade-health telemetry); pure arithmetic over injected records, 'no external aggregator required' — no LLM and no DB read exercised by the oracle (injected records).

AC-QGATE-13.why: Reads the four pinned config defaults (§3.2.2 rate 0.1, always-check set, gate model, escalation tier) from a fresh config — pure config-default assertion, no external system.

AC-QGATE-14.why: Latency measurement that gate adds no synchronous applier-hot-path cost (§3.2.2 'sampling is off the hot path'); times applier.commit() gate on vs off — in-process timing, no live LLM needed.

AC-EVENT-01.why: Emitter fires the seven material-change event types to a MOCK Doc 04 sink (§3.5); state-machine assertion on emitted-event counts — in-process pipe, no external system.

AC-EVENT-02.why: Event-payload shape (entry + focused context slice, §3.5 'each event carries the entry + a focused context slice'); contract-schema check on the emitted event object — pure shape assertion.

AC-EVENT-03.why: Chitchat delta emits zero events (§3.5 'Chitchat and running-context updates emit nothing'); mock-sink count assertion — in-process negative frontier.

AC-EVENT-04.why: Running-context update emits zero events (§3.5 'running-context updates emit nothing'); mock-sink count assertion — in-process negative frontier.

AC-EVENT-05.why: Scribe/emitter is a pipe not the mouth (§3.5 'never the mouth'); asserts speak/send_chat/show_screen call_count==0 with room-delivery surfaces mocked — in-process, no external system.

AC-EVENT-06.why: Chat record line is deterministic-formatter output, no wake, no model call (§3.5 'deterministic harness formatter ... never model-generated'); asserts wake/LLM mocks call_count==0 and byte-identical formatter output — pure determinism check.

AC-EVENT-07.why: action-item-created deterministic formatter variant (§3.5 '☐ Action: Sam ...'); asserts template match with wake/LLM mocks at zero — pure formatter assertion, no external system.

AC-EVENT-08.why: Dedupe by meeting_revision — re-fold of same committed delta emits no duplicate line (§3.5 'dedupes by meeting_revision'); simulation-trace post-count assertion, pure in-process dedupe logic.

AC-EVENT-09.why: Disable toggle suppresses all chat record posts (§3.5 'honors the room's disable toggle'); post-count==0 assertion — pure toggle branch, no external system.
